fields.summary: Support multi-homed network for docker container

status.description: A resolution has been taken, and it is awaiting verification by reporter. From here issues are either reopened, or are closed.
status.name: Resolved

issuetype.description: An improvement or enhancement to an existing feature or task.
issuetype.name: Improvement

fields.description: Docker network can be defined as configuration properties - docker.network to setup docker container to connect to a specific network in YARN service.  Docker can run multi-homed network by specifying --net=bridge --net=private-net.  This is useful to expose small number of  front end container and ports, while the rest of the infrastructure runs in private network.  This task is to add support for specifying multiple docker networks to YARN service and docker support.

Should we use "docker network connect" instead?

[~yuan_zac] Have you tried bridge and overlay network as a combination?  I tested this using Docker 18.06.1-ce and it works as designed.  Calico was design on top of layer 3 network.  It does not emulate the data link layer (Layer 2), this could be the reason that Calico enabled container doesn't see the bridged network device.

Base on conversation with [~billie.rinaldi], docker container multi-homed network only works in one combination rule:

bridge + other (where other != host)

Bridge network must be the first network to be specified, otherwise, it doesn't work.  I opened [docker ticket|https://github.com/docker/for-linux/issues/542] to make sure that Docker intends to support multi-homed networks properly.  For my next patch, if more than one network is given, container-executor will verify both networks using the combination rule above.
[~billie.rinaldi] Patch 005 added the new network configuration check, and updated documentation for multi-homed network.

[~eyang] thanks for the patch.  I have a few comments on the code:
 # DockerLinuxContainerRuntime.validateContainerNetworkType()
This should print the given network string if it fails because too many networks were specified.
This whole function would be simpler if you just start by splitting the string.  The split will return the full string if there are no commas.
 # docker-util.c - check_network_config()
I don’t think we should be doing the inspect call if there is only one network specified.
This is a change from previous code, and adds an extra docker invocation on every container launch.
I think this function should early out if the number of network names is not equal to 2.  This should make the code after the loop much simpler.

 # docker-util.c add_ports_mapping_to_command()
(nit) would be nice to extract the part of check_network_config() that does the inspects and then maybe returns a list of network types so it can be used here and in check_network_config().

Docker does not support multiple network interface using docker CLI.  What I observed via docker run --net=bridge --net=overlay was wrong.  When overlay network is specified, docker will automatically add bridge network interface in addition to overlay network interface.  

Docker run with multiple --net parameter is still a problem in [Docker  35543|https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/35543].  We may revisit this when Docker add support for multiple --net parameter.

[~eyang] thanks for the patch. :)

I have a question to ask. If docker do not support multiple networks, Is it necessary to limit the port type processing when the network type is bridge?
If only one parallel network is specified, is it also possible to have the -P parameter take effect?

https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-8986?focusedCommentId=16695628&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-16695628

[~liuxun323] We can change the check from bridge type only to bridge and overlay type.  Host network and none network doesn't need to add -P parameter.  Let's open another JIRA to add overlay type to also expose ports.

[~Jim_Brennan] {quote}So should this Jira be closed as invalid?{quote}

Yes for now.  We can open new jira to enhance this when Docker fixed their side.

Base on conversation with [~billie.rinaldi], docker container multi-homed network only works in one combination rule:

bridge + other (where other != host)

Bridge network must be the first network to be specified, otherwise, it doesn't work.  I opened [docker ticket|https://github.com/docker/for-linux/issues/542] to make sure that Docker intends to support multi-homed networks properly.  For my next patch, if more than one network is given, container-executor will verify both networks using the combination rule above.
[~billie.rinaldi] Patch 005 added the new network configuration check, and updated documentation for multi-homed network.

[~eyang] thanks for the patch.  I have a few comments on the code:
 # DockerLinuxContainerRuntime.validateContainerNetworkType()
This should print the given network string if it fails because too many networks were specified.
This whole function would be simpler if you just start by splitting the string.  The split will return the full string if there are no commas.
 # docker-util.c - check_network_config()
I don’t think we should be doing the inspect call if there is only one network specified.
This is a change from previous code, and adds an extra docker invocation on every container launch.
I think this function should early out if the number of network names is not equal to 2.  This should make the code after the loop much simpler.

 # docker-util.c add_ports_mapping_to_command()
(nit) would be nice to extract the part of check_network_config() that does the inspects and then maybe returns a list of network types so it can be used here and in check_network_config().

Docker does not support multiple network interface using docker CLI.  What I observed via docker run --net=bridge --net=overlay was wrong.  When overlay network is specified, docker will automatically add bridge network interface in addition to overlay network interface.  

Docker run with multiple --net parameter is still a problem in [Docker  35543|https://github.com/moby/moby/issues/35543].  We may revisit this when Docker add support for multiple --net parameter.

[~eyang] thanks for the patch. :)

I have a question to ask. If docker do not support multiple networks, Is it necessary to limit the port type processing when the network type is bridge?
If only one parallel network is specified, is it also possible to have the -P parameter take effect?

https://issues.apache.org/jira/browse/YARN-8986?focusedCommentId=16695628&page=com.atlassian.jira.plugin.system.issuetabpanels%3Acomment-tabpanel#comment-16695628

[~liuxun323] We can change the check from bridge type only to bridge and overlay type.  Host network and none network doesn't need to add -P parameter.  Let's open another JIRA to add overlay type to also expose ports.

[~Jim_Brennan] {quote}So should this Jira be closed as invalid?{quote}

Yes for now.  We can open new jira to enhance this when Docker fixed their side.
